summary.en: The original site of Fernandina, this quiet, grid-planned neighborhood under a canopy of live oaks feels preserved in time, retaining the atmosphere of the old Spanish settlement it once was.
The town's Victorian-era commercial and civic heart, where grander, ornate architecture reflects the prosperity of the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
Located on the northern tip of Amelia Island, this area is where preserved military history meets the wild, natural beauty of coastal Florida, from the formidable brick fort to windswept dunes.

name.en: Captain Bell's House (Pippi Longstocking's Villa Villekulla)

description.en: A piece of pop culture history, this private residence on Estrada Street served as the exterior for Pippi's chaotic home in the 1988 film.
One of the last remaining Spanish plazas in Florida, this green space was the original parade ground for the Spanish fort that once protected the settlement.

name.en: Original Town of Fernandina Historic Site

description.en: This designated site encompasses the neighborhood's Spanish-platted grid, one of the last of its kind, making a walk here a stroll through a living blueprint of colonial town planning.

name.en: United States Post Office, Custom House, and Courthouse (1912)

description.en: A superb example of early 20th-century federal architecture, this stately building speaks to Fernandina's historic importance as a port and center of commerce.
An architectural gem with a prominent clock tower, this active courthouse has been a symbol of justice and community for generations.
A striking example of Italianate architecture with a distinctive tower, this home represents the wealth and ambition of Fernandina's Gilded Age.
One of the most well-preserved 19th-century forts in the U.S., this massive brick structure offers a tangible connection to the Civil War era.
A dense, shaded canopy of ancient, moss-draped live oaks creates a peaceful, atmospheric escape with an almost magical environment.
A wild and untouched shoreline featuring a long jetty that offers panoramic views and chances to spot dolphins and sea turtles.

prompt.en: Capture the straight lines and right angles of the historic Spanish street grid in a single photo.
Find the most impressive live oak tree and take a photo looking up through its sprawling, ancient branches.
Frame a shot that captures a view of the Amelia River between two historic houses or through a gap in the trees.
Find a small, weathered detail on a historic home—like a rusty hinge, faded paint, or unique address number—and take a close-up photo.
Take a selfie on a quiet street, capturing the peaceful, preserved-in-time atmosphere of Old Town.
Find two houses with distinctly different historical architectural styles and photograph them side-by-side if possible.
Capture the long shadows cast by the live oaks across a quiet residential street.
Photograph an inviting-looking porch that captures the slow-paced, residential feel of the neighborhood.
Find a feature that reminds you of the area's Spanish colonial past and take a photo.
Peer over a historic fence (respectfully!) and capture a photo of a hidden garden or yard.
Take a photo of a street sign. Do the names (like Estrada) hint at the area's history?
Record a 10-second video capturing the quiet, ambient sounds of the neighborhood.
Find an example of ornate Victorian architecture and take a close-up of its most interesting detail, like gingerbread trim or a fancy bracket.
Create a photo that highlights the texture and color of the historic brickwork on a grand downtown building.
Take a photo of a grand civic building (like a courthouse or post office) that embodies the town's historic importance.
Find a modern element (like a car, a new sign, or a reflection in a window) contrasting with a historic building facade.
Capture a moment that shows the bustling energy of the commercial historic core.
Take a photo of the skyline created by the varied rooflines of the historic downtown buildings.
Take a selfie in front of the most ornate and impressive building you can find.
Photograph an interesting display in a storefront window along the main street.
Search for a cornerstone or plaque on a historic building and take a photo of the date it was built.
Find a building with a perfectly symmetrical facade and capture it in a head-on shot.
Find an old, painted sign on the side of a brick building or a classic-looking business sign.
Take a photo that captures the difference in feeling between the bustling downtown and the quiet Old Town.
Find a spot where wild nature is growing on or near a man-made structure (like the fort or a fence) and capture the contrast.
Take an atmospheric photo focusing on the Spanish moss hanging from the ancient live oaks in the maritime hammock.
Create a photo focusing on three different natural textures found on the beach: sand, a shell, and driftwood.
Use a part of the fort's structure, like a window, cannon embrasure, or archway, to frame a view of the surrounding landscape.
Take a selfie on the beach or jetty, letting the coastal wind add some drama to the shot.
Take a wide, panoramic photo from the jetty or a high point on the dunes.
Capture the way sunlight filters through the dense canopy of the maritime hammock, creating patterns of light and shadow on the trail.
Find a gnarled, twisted oak branch or a piece of weathered driftwood that shows the resilience of nature in this coastal environment.
Take a photo that emphasizes the massive scale and formidable brickwork of Fort Clinch.
Find the most interesting or unique seashell on the beach and take a close-up photo of it in the sand.
Take a photo from your perspective looking down one of the hiking or biking trails through the forest.
Record a 15-second video capturing the sound of the waves crashing against the shore or the jetty rocks.
